title: Door Lock Error
shortDescription: The washer door lock mechanism has failed to engage.
fullDescription: The E1 error code on TCL washers indicates the door lock assembly cannot secure the door within the expected time. The control board sends a lock signal and if confirmation is not received, E1 triggers.

causes.cause: Wiring fault between lock and board

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect door seal and latch for debris or clothing preventing closure.

fixSteps.title: Verify alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Close door slowly, observe latch. Tighten hinge screws if misaligned.

fixSteps.title: Test door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug. Access door lock. Test continuity with multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Remove old lock, install replacement, reconnect wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the control board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: TCL washer E1 error door lock fix

faq.q: How do I unlock the door with E1?
faq.a: Unplug for 3 minutes. Try the emergency release behind the drain filter panel.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E1?
faq.a: Yes. A power reset may resolve temporary glitches.

faq.q: Are TCL washer parts widely available?
faq.a: TCL parts are available through authorized dealers and online appliance parts retailers.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer cannot drain water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: The E2 error code indicates the washer failed to drain within approximately 8 minutes. Clogged filter, blocked hose, or failed pump are typical causes.

fixSteps.title: Clean drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open filter panel at bottom. Place towels below. Unscrew filter, clean thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for kinks and blockages.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With filter out, check for objects blocking impeller.

fixSteps.title: Test drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug and test pump resistance (5-20 ohms).

videoSearchQuery: TCL washer E2 error drain fix

faq.a: Use the drain filter at the bottom front.
Yes. Use HE detergent in recommended amounts.

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: Monthly.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The washer did not fill with water in the expected time.
fullDescription: The E3 error code means the machine did not detect required water level within approximately 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check supply valves
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure both valves are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect hoses, clean mesh screens.

fixSteps.title: Check hoses for kinks
fixSteps.instruction: Straighten any bends.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Test solenoid resistance (500-1500 ohms).

videoSearchQuery: TCL washer E3 error water fill fix

faq.a: Unplug for 1 minute. Error clears if issue is resolved.
Minimum 20 PSI.

faq.q: Why does E3 happen only on hot cycles?
faq.a: The hot water inlet may be clogged.

title: Overflow Error
shortDescription: Water level has exceeded the safe maximum.
fullDescription: The E4 error code indicates the water level has risen above the safe threshold. A stuck inlet valve or faulty pressure sensor is typically responsible.

fixSteps.title: Shut off water
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off supply valves immediately.

fixSteps.title: Check pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Verify connected and not kinked.

fixSteps.title: Test pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Blow into port, listen for click.

fixSteps.title: Check inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With washer unplugged, check for water dripping through.

whenToCallPro: If inlet valve is stuck open.
videoSearchQuery: TCL washer E4 error overflow fix

faq.a: Yes. Turn off water immediately.

faq.q: Can a clogged hose cause E4?
faq.a: Yes.

faq.q: Which to replace first, valve or sensor?
faq.a: Check if water flows through unplugged valve first.

title: Motor Error
shortDescription: The drive motor is not functioning properly.
fullDescription: The E5 error code indicates the motor cannot start, stalls, or runs at wrong speeds.

fixSteps.title: Reduce load
fixSteps.instruction: Remove items to reduce load size.

fixSteps.title: Check for jammed objects
fixSteps.instruction: Rotate drum by hand, listen for scraping.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Access motor. Replace if brushes shorter than 1 cm.

fixSteps.title: Test motor
fixSteps.instruction: Test windings and hall sensor with multimeter.

whenToCallPro: If motor or inverter needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: TCL washer E5 error motor fix

faq.a: Yes. Follow weight limits.
Yes. Unplug immediately.
Newer models may use BLDC inverter motors without brushes.

title: Heating Error
shortDescription: The water heater has not reached target temperature.
fullDescription: The E6 error code indicates the heater failed to heat water to the set temperature within expected time.

fixSteps.title: Test heater
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug. Test resistance (20-40 ohms). Check for ground fault.

fixSteps.title: Test NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test resistance (20K-50K ohms at room temp).

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect and reseat all connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty part
fixSteps.instruction: Replace heater or sensor as identified.

whenToCallPro: If not comfortable with electrical work near water.
videoSearchQuery: TCL washer E6 error heater fix

faq.a: Cold cycles may work but heater should be repaired.
Yes. Can trip breakers and pose shock risk.
15-30 minutes.

title: Motor Speed Sensor Error
shortDescription: The motor speed sensor is not returning correct signals.
fullDescription: The E7 error code indicates the control board cannot read motor speed accurately.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 2 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect and reseat motor connections.

fixSteps.title: Test hall sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test resistance.

fixSteps.title: Inspect rotor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and check magnets.

videoSearchQuery: TCL washer E7 error speed sensor fix

faq.a: Usually yes, mounted on the stator.
A stalled drum can cause erratic readings.

faq.q: Do I need a full motor?
faq.a: Replace hall sensor first.

title: Unbalanced Load Error
fullDescription: The UE error code indicates the washer tried multiple redistribution cycles and cannot balance the load for spin.

fixSteps.title: Redistribute load
fixSteps.instruction: Open door, untangle and spread items evenly.

fixSteps.title: Add balancing items
fixSteps.instruction: Add towels for single heavy items.

fixSteps.title: Level washer
fixSteps.instruction: Use bubble level and adjust feet.

fixSteps.title: Inspect suspension
fixSteps.instruction: Check shock absorbers if UE is frequent.

whenToCallPro: If UE occurs frequently with all loads.
videoSearchQuery: TCL washer UE error unbalanced fix

faq.q: Can a single blanket cause UE?
faq.a: Yes. Add towels to balance.
Yes, anti-vibration pads reduce movement.
Occasional UE is normal. Frequent events accelerate wear.

title: Overflow Protection Triggered
shortDescription: The base flood sensor detected water in the bottom of the washer.
fullDescription: The E9 error code is triggered when the anti-flood sensor detects water accumulation in the base pan, indicating an internal leak.

fixSteps.title: Check for leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water, unplug, inspect underneath.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check for tears or debris in gasket.

fixSteps.title: Check internal hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten clamps, check for cracks.

fixSteps.title: Dry base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt washer, dry with towels, verify float drops.

whenToCallPro: If tub is cracked or leak source unknown.
videoSearchQuery: TCL washer E9 error leak fix

faq.a: Yes, but it returns if the leak is not fixed.
Yes. Address promptly to prevent water damage.
Centrifugal force pushes water through small gaps.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: Main control and display boards cannot communicate.
fullDescription: The F1 error code indicates communication failure between the main and display boards.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Reseat cable
fixSteps.instruction: Open top panel, reseat ribbon cable.

fixSteps.title: Clean connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Check for corrosion, clean with isopropyl alcohol.

fixSteps.title: Replace board
fixSteps.instruction: Replace display board first, then main board if needed.

whenToCallPro: If reseating connectors does not resolve.
videoSearchQuery: TCL washer F1 error communication board fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F1?
faq.a: Yes. Use a surge protector.

faq.q: Replace display or main board first?
faq.a: Start with the less expensive display board.
Usually indicates a loose connection.
